title: cephaelis hemicephaelis
intro: Cephaelis hemicephaelis, also known as the Half-White Woodrose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. Cephaelis hemicephaelis can be prepared as a decoction or infusion by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, and is also used in some herbal remedies to treat conditions such as fever and respiratory issues.
